ab: ihaan dusa-singing tu mapusan qan dusa qamisan, sihu tau’aniv Pali’uni takiSilazan mivunsiu maqtu pintas’aun siduq ngan tu paliqansiap.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: mintivi masingav i malbuntu malka sihu taidangazsiza mivunsiu, paltas’aun madailaz a siduq ngan, tu asang mainduduaz Sipal.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Sipal。
en: I interviewed Sipal, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: paitupa’in aam ihaan haip pal’uun tu diqanin munhaan isiataa lumaq masingav istaa.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: ihaan tantungu tu aingqaiban, malka zaku aipa taqu maimaquaq isiataa tu ngan, dasun aak mapasaqal isiataa tu tastulumaq, isiataa asang.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: masingav aak istaa mavia masaa tu asa huvaivan a ngan, tatu’un tu sinpatuki patasan nii tu masihal?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: malka zaku aipa tupa, aupa makumalasTaluu mapatas nii maqtu mamantuk taqu ki isiataa tu ngan, duma bunun maldaukang luklas i siata tu “si’sien’sen”, paqpun minhaiza pinqainanun i bunun.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「西先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Xi Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: tupa Sipal tu, takiSilazan qalinga ispapatas tu ngan sia imita mamantuk tu ngan. Inaak tu ngan min’uni inaam tu siduq, inaam tu tastuqabu, inaam tu hubungsinpangqal, maqtu’ang haiza madia siduq tais’aan ukiliman sisuqaisun tu ngan.
ch: Sipal說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Sipal said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
